summary:
A mediolateral episiotomy reduces the likelihood of deep tissue injury by creating better-controlled tears limited primarily to skin and subcutaneous tissues rather than potentially involving muscles with a midline approach. This leads to fewer complications from excessive surgical cutting during delivery. \boxed{Mediolateral Episiotomy Advantage}

The summary captures the essential point regarding control of tear depth as opposed to potential extensive damage seen with midline incisions while staying within two concise statements focused directly on clinically-relevant aspects related to performing this procedure safely under specified conditions. Agent2's initial response highlighted one specific benefit but did not fully capture both core elements requested; thus, it was expanded upon here maintaining alignment closely with provided requirements yet enhancing clarity around comparative outcomes between techniques used commonly in obstetric practice scenarios described initially.
